title: Bradycardia documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.
examination.rationale: The ACC/AHA/HRS algorithm supports documenting symptoms, comprehensive history and physical examination, vital signs, cardiovascular examination, ECG, selected existing tests, medication and reversible-cause context without automatically diagnosing a conduction disorder.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

source.title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of Bradycardia and Conduction Disease Algorithm
exact_section.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF page 9, Figure 1, plus page 10 reversible-cause table

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting symptom-rhythm correlation, symptom frequency, structural-disease context, reviewed ECG or ambulatory monitoring, and observed follow-up when appropriate; pacing and treatment branches are not mapped.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The chronic algorithms support recording symptom-rhythm correlation, monitoring context, and clinician-entered follow-up without generating pacing, treatment, or urgency decisions.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting symptom-rhythm correlation, symptom frequency, structural-disease context, reviewed ECG or ambulatory monitoring, and observed follow-up when appropriate; pacing and treatment branches are not mapped.

source.title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple

exact_section.heading: Chronic bradycardia and conduction-disorder algorithms
exact_section.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF pages 11-13 and 23-24

source_limitations: Actual heart rate and rhythm, symptom correlation, onset and triggers, medicines, reversible causes, ECG findings, laboratory results, structural disease, monitor result, diagnosis, instability, treatment, pacing, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
